testcase.name: test_hyperedge
testcase.failure:
self = <ash_model.test.test_ash.ASHTestCase testMethod=test_hyperedge>
def test_hyperedge(self): a = ASH(hedge_removal=True) a.add_hyperedge([1, 2, 3], 0, 1) a.add_hyperedge([1, 2, 3], 6, 9) a.add_hyperedge([1, 2, 3], -3, -2) a.add_hyperedge([3, 4, 5], 3, 4)
self.assertEqual(a.get_avg_number_of_hyperedges(), 1.0) self.assertEqual(a.hyperedge_contribution("e1"), 0.8)
self.assertEqual(a.has_hyperedge([1, 2, 3]), True) self.assertEqual(a.has_hyperedge([1, 2, 4]), False) self.assertEqual(a.has_hyperedge([1, 2, 3], tid=0), True) self.assertEqual(a.has_hyperedge([1, 2, 3], tid=5), False)
self.assertEqual(a.get_size(), 2) self.assertEqual(a.get_size(0), 1)
> self.assertEqual(a.get_number_of_neighbors(1), 3) E AssertionError: 2 != 3
ash_model/test/test_ash.py:191: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_node_shortest_s_walk
testcase.failure:
self = <ash_model.test.test_s_walks.SWalksCase testMethod=test_node_shortest_s_walk>
def test_node_shortest_s_walk(self): a = self.get_hypergraph() s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 2, 1, 4, edge=False) self.assertEqual(len(s_w), 2) s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 3, 1, 4, edge=False) self.assertEqual(len(s_w), 2)
s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 2, 1, 4, weight=True, edge=False) self.assertEqual(len(s_w), 2)
s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 2, 1, 4, start=1, end=1, weight=True, edge=False) self.assertEqual(len(s_w), 0)
s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 1, 1, edge=False) self.assertDictEqual(s_w, {1: [1], 2: [1, 2], 3: [1, 3], 4: [1, 4]})
> s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 2, edge=False) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
ash_model/test/test_s_walks.py:163: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ash_model/paths/walks.py:233: in shortest_s_walk return [eid_to_node[i] for i in res] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
.0 = <generator object all_pairs_shortest_path at 0x12415a040>
> return [eid_to_node[i] for i in res] ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ E TypeError: unhashable type: 'dict'
ash_model/paths/walks.py:233: TypeError

testcase.name: test_shortest_s_walk

failure.message: AssertionError: <generator object all_pairs_shortest_path at 0x12415b840> is not an instance of <class 'dict'> : First argument is not a dictionary

testcase.failure:
self = <ash_model.test.test_s_walks.SWalksCase testMethod=test_shortest_s_walk>
def test_shortest_s_walk(self): a = self.get_hypergraph() s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 2, "e1", "e2") self.assertEqual(len(s_w), 3) s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 3, "e1", "e2") self.assertEqual(len(s_w), 0)
s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 2, "e1", "e2", weight=True) self.assertEqual(len(s_w), 3)
s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 2, "e1") self.assertDictEqual( s_w, { "e1": ["e1"], "e3": ["e1", "e3"], "e4": ["e1", "e4"], "e2": ["e1", "e3", "e2"], "e5": ["e1", "e3", "e5"], }, )
s_w = shortest_s_walk(a, 2) > self.assertDictEqual( s_w, { "e1": { "e1": ["e1"], "e3": ["e1", "e3"], "e4": ["e1", "e4"], "e2": ["e1", "e3", "e2"], "e5": ["e1", "e3", "e5"], }, "e3": { "e3": ["e3"], "e1": ["e3", "e1"], "e2": ["e3", "e2"], "e4": ["e3", "e4"], "e5": ["e3", "e5"], }, "e4": { "e4": ["e4"], "e1": ["e4", "e1"], "e2": ["e4", "e2"], "e3": ["e4", "e3"], "e5": ["e4", "e5"], }, "e2": { "e2": ["e2"], "e3": ["e2", "e3"], "e4": ["e2", "e4"], "e1": ["e2", "e3", "e1"], "e5": ["e2", "e3", "e5"], }, "e5": { "e5": ["e5"], "e3": ["e5", "e3"], "e4": ["e5", "e4"], "e1": ["e5", "e3", "e1"], "e2": ["e5", "e3", "e2"], }, }, ) E AssertionError: <generator object all_pairs_shortest_path at 0x12415b840> is not an instance of <class 'dict'> : First argument is not a dictionary
ash_model/test/test_s_walks.py:43: AssertionError

testcase.name: test_plot_degree_distribution

failure.message:
ValueError: Invalid value of type 'builtins.range' received for the 'x' property of scatter Received value: range(0, 5)
The 'x' property is an array that may be specified as a tuple, list, numpy array, or pandas Series

testcase.failure:
self = <ash_model.test.test_static_viz.StaticVizTestCase testMethod=test_plot_degree_distribution>
def test_plot_degree_distribution(self): a = self.get_hypergraph() > ax = plot_degree_distribution(a) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
ash_model/test/test_static_viz.py:31: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ ash_model/viz/static.py:108: in plot_degree_distribution go.Scatter( .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/plotly/graph_objs/_scatter.py:2823: in __init__ self._set_property("x", arg, x) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/plotly/basedatatypes.py:4403: in _set_property _set_property_provided_value(self, name, arg, provided) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/plotly/basedatatypes.py:398: in _set_property_provided_value obj[name] = val ^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/plotly/basedatatypes.py:4932: in __setitem__ self._set_prop(prop, value) .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/plotly/basedatatypes.py:5276: in _set_prop raise err .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/plotly/basedatatypes.py:5271: in _set_prop val = validator.validate_coerce(val) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ .venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/_plotly_utils/basevalidators.py:424: in validate_coerce self.raise_invalid_val(v) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
self = <_plotly_utils.basevalidators.DataArrayValidator object at 0x1253a9410> v = range(0, 5), inds = None
def raise_invalid_val(self, v, inds=None): """ Helper method to raise an informative exception when an invalid value is passed to the validate_coerce method.
Parameters ---------- v : Value that was input to validate_coerce and could not be coerced inds: list of int or None (default) Indexes to display after property name. e.g. if self.plotly_name is 'prop' and inds=[2, 1] then the name in the validation error message will be 'prop[2][1]` Raises ------- ValueError """ name = self.plotly_name if inds: for i in inds: name += "[" + str(i) + "]"
> raise ValueError( """ Invalid value of type {typ} received for the '{name}' property of {pname} Received value: {v}
{valid_clr_desc}""".format( name=name, pname=self.parent_name, typ=type_str(v), v=repr(v), valid_clr_desc=self.description(), ) ) E ValueError: E Invalid value of type 'builtins.range' received for the 'x' property of scatter E Received value: range(0, 5) E E The 'x' property is an array that may be specified as a tuple, E list, numpy array, or pandas Series
.venv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/_plotly_utils/basevalidators.py:312: ValueError

testcase.name: test_plot_structure_dynamics
